page: Microsoft Azure DC1ds_v3 is a virtual machine belonging to the DDCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a single dedicated x86_64 vCPU powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. This single-core, single-thread configuration is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. The processor includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI) and cryptographic acceleration (AES, VAES, SHA_NI). Local storage consists of a 77 GB drive, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory throughput. The single vCPU architecture achieves average ratings in single-core stress-ng (1879.39) and Geekbench single-core (1626.0) tests, as well as single-threaded gzip compression (28.36 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (10.36 GB/sec). However, due to the single-core limitation, multi-core benchmarks like Geekbench multi-core (1645.0) and stress-ng multi-core fall into the bottom 10% tier. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read-only and write speeds registering at 13.71 GB/sec and 11.19 GB/sec respectively. Database operations and static web serving throughput are similarly limited, with Redis SET operations reaching 2.19 million operations per second and web serving throughput at 2.68 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the DC1ds_v3 provides a dedicated Intel Xeon core and a relatively high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 without the overhead of multi-core licensing or higher-tier pricing. This makes it a resource-dense option for lightweight workloads requiring dedicated CPU execution and moderate memory capacity rather than raw parallel processing power. It is best suited for basic single-threaded applications, lightweight text processing, small-scale database operations, or low-demand utility services that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation and local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure DC1ds_v3 is a single-core virtual machine from the DDCSv3 family, utilizing a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8.0 GB of RAM, a 77 GB local storage drive, and support for Premium Storage. The x86_64 architecture includes AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction sets. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, such as a Geekbench single-core score of 1626.0 and efficient single-threaded compression, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are limited due to the single-vCPU constraint. Qualitatively, this server offers a high memory-to-core ratio for a single-vCPU instance, making it a cost-efficient choice for workloads that do not require parallel processing. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and small utility services.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC1ds_v3 is a single-vCPU virtual machine featuring a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 77 GB of local storage, optimized for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

tagline: A single-core virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-CPU ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon performance for lightweight utility workloads.

8.0 GB of system memory providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
77 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average single-core performance for single-threaded tasks and text processing
Suited for lightweight utility services, basic databases, and single-threaded applications
